title: Order Relation Hierarchy in Cardinal Numbers
description: A visual representation of how cardinal numbers can be ordered based on the existence of injective functions between sets, leading to concepts like equality, comparability, and the Schröder-Bernstein Theorem.
relationships: |A| ≤ |B| is defined by existence of f
For any two sets, either |A| ≤ |B| or |B| ≤ |A| (assuming Axiom of Choice)
